back_translation: When Jesus knew that the Pharisees had heard that He had disciples and gave baptism more than John
(Although Jesus Himself did not give baptism, but it was His disciples who gave [it])
He therefore departed from the region of Judea, and went back to the region of Galilee
He had to pass through the region of Samaria
He therefore came to a town in the region of Samaria called Sychar, near the plot of land that Jacob had given to Joseph his son
Jacob's well was there. Jesus, weary from the journey, sat down beside that well. The time then was about noon
A Samaritan woman came to draw water. Jesus said to her, 'Please give Me a drink'
(Because His disciples had gone into the town to buy food)
The Samaritan woman therefore said to Him, 'You are a Jew; how is it that You ask for a drink from me, who am a Samaritan woman?' (For Jews do not associate with Samaritans)
Jesus answered her, 'If you knew the gift of God, and knew who this is who asks for a drink from you, you would have been the one asking from Him, and He would have given you living water'
The woman said to Him, 'Sir, You have no bucket to draw water with, and this well is deep. From where then will You get that living water?
Are You greater than Jacob our forefather, who gave this well to us, and he himself drank from this well, both his sons and his herds of livestock also drank?'
Jesus answered her, 'Everyone who drinks this water will be thirsty again
But whoever drinks the water that I will give him, that one will not be thirsty again forever. The water that I will give him will become a spring inside him, gushing out to eternal life'
The woman said to Him, 'Sir, please give that water to me, that I may not be thirsty and may not have to come draw water here again'
Jesus said to her, 'Go call your husband and come here'
The woman answered, 'I have no husband.' Jesus said to her, 'You spoke rightly that ‘I have no husband’
Because you have had five husbands, and the one you are with at this time is also not your husband. What you have spoken is the truth'
The woman said to Him, 'Sir, I see that You are a prophet
Our forefathers worshiped on this mountain, but you Jews say that Jerusalem is the place where one must worship'
Jesus said to her, 'O woman, please believe Me, the time is coming when you (pl.) will worship the Father not at this mountain and not at Jerusalem
You worship what you do not know, but we worship what we know, because salvation comes from the Jews
But the time will come, and at this very moment is already here, when the true worshipers will worship the Father with spirit and truth, because the Father seeks such people to worship Him
God is Spirit, and those who worship Him must worship with spirit and truth'
The woman said to Him, 'I know that the Messiah (the one who is called the Christ) will come. When He comes, He will proclaim everything to us'
Jesus said to her, 'I, who am speaking with you here, am that very One'
At that very moment His disciples returned, and were astonished that He was conversing with that woman. But no one humbly asked, 'What does He want?' or 'Why is He conversing with her?'
The woman therefore left her water-jar there, and returned into the town, and said to the townspeople,
'Come, come see a certain man who told me about everything that I have done. Could this one be the Christ?'
They therefore went out from the town and were coming to Him
In the meantime, the disciples entreated Him, 'Rabbi, please eat'
But He said to them, 'I have food to eat that you do not know'
The disciples therefore said among themselves, 'Has anyone brought food for Him?'
Jesus said to them, 'My food is doing the will of Him who sent Me, and completing the royal-work of His
Do you not say, ‘Yet four months until the harvest season comes’? Behold, I tell you, lift up your face and look at the fields; those fields are golden-bright, ready for harvest already
The reaper receives wages and gathers fruit into eternal life, so that both the sower and the reaper will rejoice together
In this case the saying ‘one sows, another reaps’ is true
I sent you (pl.) to reap what you did not labor for. Others have labored, and you have entered into the labor of those ones'
Many Samaritans from that town believed in Him, because of the testimony of that woman that 'He told me about everything that I have done'
When the Samaritans came to Him, they entreated Him to stay with them, and He stayed there two days
And there were even more who believed because of His [royal] word
They said to that woman, 'Now we believe not because of your words anymore, because we ourselves have heard and know that this very One is truly the Savior of the world'
After those two days, He went out from there to the region of Galilee
Because Jesus Himself testified that a prophet does not receive honor in his own homeland

When He arrived in the region of Galilee, the Galileans welcomed Him, because they had seen all the things He did in Jerusalem during that festival (because they themselves had also gone to that festival)
He therefore went again to the town of Cana in the region of Galilee, where He had turned water into wine. There was a certain royal-official whose son was sick at the town of Capernaum

When this royal-official heard that Jesus had come from the region of Judea to the region of Galilee, he therefore went into His presence and humbly entreated, asking Him to come down and heal his son, because that son was about to die
Jesus therefore said to him, 'If you (pl.) do not see signs and wonders, you will not believe at all'
That royal-official said to Him, 'Sir, please come down before my child dies'
Jesus said to him, 'Go now; your son still has life.' That man believed the [royal] word that Jesus spoke to him, and so returned
While that royal-official was traveling down, his servants came to meet him, and reported that his child still has life
He therefore asked them about the time that the son began to get better. They answered, 'Yesterday at one in the afternoon the fever left him'
The father then knew that it was the same time when Jesus said to him, 'Your son still has life.' Then he and all in his household believed
This was the second sign that Jesus did when He came from the region of Judea to the region of Galilee
